steps.title: Identify the contrast between the texts
steps.content: The defining signal is the gap between Text 1's optimistic framing—the EGR discovery is “especially promising”—and Text 2's qualification: the gene “likely functions very differently” between humans and worms, and human function “remains unclear.”

steps.title: Judge Text 1's characterization from Text 2's perspective
steps.content:
Text 2 would deem Text 1's assessment overly optimistic, because while the shared gene is noteworthy, Text 2 emphasizes the limitations: the gene works very differently, and its human mechanism is still unknown.

This makes Text 1's promise seem premature. Choice A wrongly claims they've identified how EGR operates in humans; the others misread Text 2's view of excitement or Text 1's tone.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
